entry.title: AI Snake Oil: What Artificial Intelligence Can Do, What It Can’t, and How to Tell the Difference
entry.quote: On April 17, 2025, the MIT Shaping the Future of Work Initiative and the MIT Schwarzman College of Computing welcomed Arvind Narayanan, Professor of Computer Science at Princeton University, to discuss his latest book, "AI Snake Oil: What Artificial Intelligence Can Do, What It Can’t, and How to Tell the Difference," co-authored with Sayash Kapoor.

entry.title: Why Even Try if You Have A.I.?
entry.quote: In a world saturated with technology, we already have to remind ourselves to put down our phones; to go outside; to see friends in person; to go places instead of staring at them on our screens; to have non-technological experiences, such as boredom. If we’re not careful, then our minds will do less as computers do more, and we will be diminished as a result.

entry.title: Off-screen in the Wilds of Manhattan
entry.quote: What a New York exhibition taught me about the costs of creative triumph.

entry.title: How to Build a Great Emerging Tech Product
entry.quote: We’re excited to adopt it to create a really great product. But how do we make it stick?

entry.title: Altair at 50: Remembering the first Personal Computer
entry.quote: No longer did you need an electronics degree to assemble an inexpensive computer or be a business that had to spend tens to hundreds of thousands of dollars (and have a staff) in order to use a computer.
